label: Breadcrumb
summary: A breadcrumb is a secondary navigation scheme that reveals the user's location in a website or web application.
overview:
- Breadcrumbs provide a trail for users to follow back to the starting or entry point.
 - They help users understand their current location within the site hierarchy.
 - Follows WCAG 2.1 guidelines for accessible navigation landmarks.

customElement.name: Breadcrumb

members.name: label
members.description: Accessible label for the breadcrumb navigation landmark.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Accessible label for the breadcrumb navigation landmark.

customElement.summary: A breadcrumb is a secondary navigation scheme that reveals the user's location in a website or web application.

members.description: Hyperlink to navigate to on click.

members.name: target
members.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

members.description: Indicates if this is the active (current) breadcrumb item.

members.name: separator

label: Button
summary: Buttons help people initiate actions, from sending an email, to sharing a document, to liking a post.
overview: Buttons are clickable elements that are used to trigger actions. They communicate calls to action to the user and allow users to interact with pages in a variety of ways. Button labels express what action will occur when the user interacts with it.

members.name: iconAlign
members.description:
Icon alignment.
Possible values are `"start"`, `"end"`. Defaults to `"end"`.

members.name: type
members.description: Type is preset of color and variant. Type will be only applied.

members.name: variant
members.description:
The visual style of the button.

 Possible variant values:
`"filled"` is a filled button.
`"outlined"` is an outlined button.
`"text"` is a transparent button.
`"tonal"` is a light color button.
`"elevated"` is elevated button

members.name: color
members.description: Defines the primary color of the button. This can be set to predefined color names to apply specific color themes.

members.name: size
members.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: softDisabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button and the button is visually styled as disabled. But the button is still focusable. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: disabledReason
members.description: If button is disabled, the reason why it is disabled.

members.name: href
members.description: Hyperlink to navigate to on click.

members.name: target
members.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

members.name: throttleDelay
members.description: Sets the delay for throttle in milliseconds. Defaults to 200 milliseconds.

members.name: isPressed
members.description: States

events.description: Dispatched when the button is clicked.
events.name: click

label: Button Group
summary: Group a series of buttons together on a single line with the button group, and super-power.
example:
```html
<wc-button-group variant="connected">
 <wc-icon-button name="home" toggle selected></wc-icon-button>
 <wc-icon-button name="alarm" toggle></wc-icon-button>
</wc-button-group>
```

customElement.name: ButtonGroup

members.name: size
members.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: variant
members.description:
Layout variant of the button group.
`"standard"` shows buttons with a small gap between them.
`"connected"` places buttons with a 2px gap; in horizontal orientation outer buttons have fully
rounded outer corners and middle buttons keep standard rounded corners. In vertical orientation
all buttons keep their default corner shape.
Defaults to `"standard"`.

members.name: orientation
members.description:
Orientation of the button group.
`"horizontal"` lays buttons out in a row.
`"vertical"` stacks buttons in a column.
Defaults to `"horizontal"`.

members.name: color
members.description:
Color applied to all buttons in the group.
Possible values are `"primary"`, `"success"`, `"danger"`, `"warning"`, `"light"`, `"dark"`.

members.name: buttonVariant
members.description:
Visual style applied to all buttons in the group.
Possible values are `"filled"`, `"tonal"`, `"outlined"`.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
Layout variant of the button group.
`"standard"` shows buttons with a small gap between them.
`"connected"` places buttons with a 2px gap; in horizontal orientation outer buttons have fully
rounded outer corners and middle buttons keep standard rounded corners. In vertical orientation
all buttons keep their default corner shape.
Defaults to `"standard"`.

attributes.name: orientation
attributes.description:
Orientation of the button group.
`"horizontal"` lays buttons out in a row.
`"vertical"` stacks buttons in a column.
Defaults to `"horizontal"`.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description:
Color applied to all buttons in the group.
Possible values are `"primary"`, `"success"`, `"danger"`, `"warning"`, `"light"`, `"dark"`.

attributes.name: button-variant
attributes.description:
Visual style applied to all buttons in the group.
Possible values are `"filled"`, `"tonal"`, `"outlined"`.

customElement.summary: Group a series of buttons together on a single line with the button group, and super-power.

label: Chart Bar
summary: A vertical bar chart that follows Material Design 3 color and spacing tokens.
example:
```html
<wc-chart-bar width="520" height="320"></wc-chart-bar>
<script>
  document.querySelector('wc-chart-bar').data = [
    { name: 'apples', label: 'Apples', value: 20 },
    { name: 'bananas', label: 'Bananas', value: 35 },
    { name: 'cherries', label: 'Cherries', value: 15 },
  ];
</script>
```

customElement.name: ChartBar

members.name: width
members.description: Width of the chart in pixels.

members.name: height
members.description: Height of the chart in pixels.

members.name: margin
members.description: Margin around the chart drawing area.

members.name: data
members.description: Chart data array. Each item should have name, value, and optional label and color.

members.name: showValues
members.description: Whether to render value labels above bars.

label: Chart Doughnut
summary: A doughnut chart is a circular chart with a blank center. The area in the center can be used to display information.
example:
```html
<wc-chart-doughnut width="400" label="Total"></wc-chart-doughnut>
<script>
  document.querySelector('wc-chart-doughnut').data = [
    { name: 'A', value: 30, label: 'Category A' },
    { name: 'B', value: 50, label: 'Category B' },
    { name: 'C', value: 20, label: 'Category C' },
  ];
</script>
```

label: Checkbox
summary: Captures boolean input with an optional indeterminate mode.
overview:
Checkboxes allow users to select one or more items from a set. Checkboxes can turn an option on or off.

Material Design 3 checkboxes feature a smooth animation and clear visual states for checked, unchecked, and indeterminate.

fires: focus - Dispatched when the checkbox receives focus.

label: Code Editor
summary: A Monaco-based code editing component with syntax highlighting and theming.
overview:
- CodeEditor wraps Monaco Editor as a web component.
 - Supports JS/JSON/HTML languages, read-only mode, line numbers, minimap, and dark/light theme.
 - Emits `change` when content is edited and updates value from property changes.
example:
```html
<wc-code-editor
  language="javascript"
  style="width: 100%; --code-editor-height: 10rem;"
  value="function hello() { console.log('Hello'); }"
  lineNumbers="on"
  minimap="false">
</wc-code-editor>
```

customElement.name: CodeEditor
customElement.summary: A Monaco-based code editing component with syntax highlighting and theming.

label: Icon Button
summary: Icon buttons allow users to take actions, and make choices, with a single tap.
overview: Icon buttons are clickable elements that are used to trigger actions. They communicate calls to action to the user and allow users to interact with pages in a variety of ways. IconButton labels express what action will occur when the user interacts with it.
cssprop: --neo-button-label-text-color: Text color of the neo button label.
fires: button:click - Dispatched when the button is clicked.

members.name: type
members.description: Type is preset of color and variant. Type will be only applied.

members.name: variant
members.description:
The visual style of the button.

 Possible variant values:
`"filled"` is a filled button.
`"outlined"` is an outlined button.
`"text"` is a transparent button.
`"tonal"` is a light color button.
`"elevated"` is elevated button

members.name: color
members.description: Defines the primary color of the button. This can be set to predefined color names to apply specific color themes.

members.name: size
members.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: softDisabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button and the button is visually styled as disabled. But the button is still focusable. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: disabledReason
members.description: If button is disabled, the reason why it is disabled.

members.name: href
members.description: Hyperlink to navigate to on click.

members.name: target
members.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

members.name: throttleDelay
members.description: Sets the delay for throttle in milliseconds. Defaults to 200 milliseconds.

members.name: isPressed
members.description: States

events.description: Dispatched when the button is clicked.
events.name: button:click

label: Ripple
summary: Provides ripple effect for interactive elements.
overview: Ripple creates a visual feedback effect when users interact with buttons or other clickable elements.

customElement.name: Ripple

members.name: disabled
members.description: Disables the ripple.

members.name: shouldReactToEvent
members.description:
Returns `true` if
 - the ripple element is enabled
 - the pointer is primary for the input type
 - the pointer is the pointer that started the interaction, or will start
the interaction
 - the pointer is a touch, or the pointer state has the primary button
held, or the pointer is hovering

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the ripple.

label: Segmented Button Group
summary: A container for segmented buttons following Material Design 3.
overview:
Segmented buttons help people select options, switch views, or sort elements. They follow the Material Design 3 specification.

Use multi-select to allow more than one segment to be selected at a time. By default only one segment can be active (single-select).
fires: change - Dispatched when the selection changes. Detail contains { value, values }.

customElement.name: SegmentedButtonGroup

cssProperties.description: Border-radius of the group container.
cssProperties.name: --segmented-button-group-shape:

members.name: multiSelect
members.description:
When true, multiple segments can be selected simultaneously.
Defaults to single-select mode.

members.name: nullable
members.description:
When true, in single-select mode the currently selected segment can be
deselected by clicking it again (allowing an empty selection).
Defaults to `false`.

events.description: Dispatched when the selection changes. Detail contains { value, values }.
events.name: change

attributes.name: multi-select
attributes.description:
When true, multiple segments can be selected simultaneously.
Defaults to single-select mode.

attributes.name: nullable
attributes.description:
When true, in single-select mode the currently selected segment can be
deselected by clicking it again (allowing an empty selection).
Defaults to `false`.

customElement.summary: A container for segmented buttons following Material Design 3.

label: Select
summary: A dropdown select component supporting single and multi-select with optional typeahead search.
overview:
Select builds on wc-field and wc-menu to provide a fully-featured dropdown picker.

- Single and multi-select modes
- Client-side typeahead with `search="contains"`
- Server-side typeahead with `search="managed"`
- Multi-select chips display
example:
```html
<wc-select label="Fruit" placeholder="Pick a fruit...">
  <wc-option value="apple">Apple</wc-option>
  <wc-option value="banana">Banana</wc-option>
</wc-select>
```

customElement.name: Select

members.name: options
members.description:
Array of options to display in the dropdown.
Setting this property creates matching `<wc-option>` children automatically.

members.name: value
members.description: The selected value. For multi-select, a comma-separated list of values.

members.name: multiple
members.description: Enable multi-select mode.

members.name: search
members.description:
Enable typeahead search.
- `'contains'`: filters options client-side.
- `'managed'`: emits a `select-search` event for server-controlled filtering.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Placeholder text shown when no value is selected.

members.name: label
members.description: Label displayed above the field.

members.name: clearable
members.description: Show a clear button in single-select mode when a value is selected.

members.name: variant
members.description: Visual variant of the field.

members.name: helperText
members.description: Helper text displayed below the field.

members.name: _noOptionsVisible
members.description: True when all options are filtered out by the current search query.

members.name: _syncProgrammaticOptions
members.description:
Reconciles the `options` property with auto-generated `<wc-option>` light-DOM
children (marked `data-generated`).  Declarative children placed by the
consumer are left untouched.

members.name: _syncOptionStates
members.description:
Pushes `selected`, `keepOpen`, and `filtered` state onto every `<wc-option>`
child element so each one can render itself correctly.

members.name: _getLabelForValue
members.description: Returns the display label for a given option value.

attributes.name: options
attributes.description:
Array of options to display in the dropdown.
Setting this property creates matching `<wc-option>` children automatically.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The selected value. For multi-select, a comma-separated list of values.

attributes.name: multiple
attributes.description: Enable multi-select mode.

attributes.name: search
attributes.description:
Enable typeahead search.
- `'contains'`: filters options client-side.
- `'managed'`: emits a `select-search` event for server-controlled filtering.

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description: Placeholder text shown when no value is selected.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Label displayed above the field.

attributes.name: clearable
attributes.description: Show a clear button in single-select mode when a value is selected.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: Visual variant of the field.

attributes.name: helper-text
attributes.description: Helper text displayed below the field.

customElement.summary: A dropdown select component supporting single and multi-select with optional typeahead search.

label: Select Option
summary: A declarative option element for use inside wc-select.

customElement.name: SelectOptionElement

members.name: value
members.description: The option's submitted value.

members.name: icon
members.description: Optional Material Symbol icon name shown before the label.

members.name: disabled
members.description: Disables the option so it cannot be selected.

members.name: selected
members.description: Reflects whether this option is currently selected. Set by wc-select.

members.name: keepOpen
members.description:
When true the menu stays open after selection (used for multi-select).
Set by wc-select.

members.name: filtered
members.description:
When true the option is hidden and excluded from keyboard navigation
because it does not match the current typeahead search query.
Set by wc-select.

members.name: item
members.description:
Returns the inner `wc-menu-item` element.
`wc-menu` discovers this via its `items` getter which checks `el.item`
as a `MenuItem` proxy, so keyboard navigation and activation work
without `wc-option` extending `MenuItem` directly.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The option's submitted value.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Optional Material Symbol icon name shown before the label.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the option so it cannot be selected.

attributes.name: selected
attributes.description: Reflects whether this option is currently selected. Set by wc-select.

attributes.name: keep-open
attributes.description:
When true the menu stays open after selection (used for multi-select).
Set by wc-select.

attributes.name: filtered
attributes.description:
When true the option is hidden and excluded from keyboard navigation
because it does not match the current typeahead search query.
Set by wc-select.

customElement.summary: A declarative option element for use inside wc-select.

label: Table
summary: A configurable component for displaying tabular data.
overview: The table component displays rows of data with support for sorting, pagination, row selection, and fixed columns.
fires: page - Dispatched when the page or page size changes.

customElement.name: Table

members.name: columns
members.description:
Grid columns configuration.
Each column can have: name, label, width (px), fixed (boolean), template (function).

members.name: data
members.description: Grid data to display on table.

members.name: selectionType
members.description: Row selection type. Set to `"checkbox"` to enable checkbox selection.

members.name: selectedRowKeys
members.description: Array of selected row key values.

members.name: keyField
members.description: The field name used as the unique key for each row. Defaults to `"id"`.

members.name: managed
members.description: If true, sorting and pagination are managed externally (controlled mode).

members.name: sortable
members.description: If true, columns are sortable. Defaults to `true`.

members.name: sortBy
members.description: The field name currently used for sorting.

members.name: sortOrder
members.description: The current sort order. Possible values are `"asc"` and `"desc"`. Defaults to `"asc"`.

members.name: paginate
members.description: If true, pagination is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

members.name: page
members.description: The current page number (1-based). Defaults to `1`.

members.name: pageSize
members.description: The number of rows per page. Defaults to `10`.

members.name: totalItems
members.description: Total number of items (used in managed/controlled mode).

members.name: emptyStateHeadline
members.description: Headline text shown when the table has no data.

members.name: emptyStateDescription
members.description: Description text shown when the table has no data.

events.name: selection-change
events.description: Dispatched when the row selection changes.

events.name: cell-click
events.description: Dispatched when a table cell is clicked.

events.name: sort
events.description: Dispatched when the table is sorted.

events.name: page
events.description: Dispatched when the page or page size changes.

attributes.name: columns
attributes.description:
Grid columns configuration.
Each column can have: name, label, width (px), fixed (boolean), template (function).

attributes.name: data
attributes.description: Grid data to display on table.

attributes.name: selection-type
attributes.description: Row selection type. Set to `"checkbox"` to enable checkbox selection.

attributes.name: selected-row-keys
attributes.description: Array of selected row key values.

attributes.name: key-field
attributes.description: The field name used as the unique key for each row. Defaults to `"id"`.

attributes.name: managed
attributes.description: If true, sorting and pagination are managed externally (controlled mode).

attributes.name: sortable
attributes.description: If true, columns are sortable. Defaults to `true`.

attributes.name: sort-by
attributes.description: The field name currently used for sorting.

attributes.name: sort-order
attributes.description: The current sort order. Possible values are `"asc"` and `"desc"`. Defaults to `"asc"`.

attributes.name: paginate
attributes.description: If true, pagination is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

attributes.name: page
attributes.description: The current page number (1-based). Defaults to `1`.

attributes.name: page-size
attributes.description: The number of rows per page. Defaults to `10`.

attributes.name: total-items
attributes.description: Total number of items (used in managed/controlled mode).

attributes.name: empty-state-headline
attributes.description: Headline text shown when the table has no data.

attributes.name: empty-state-description
attributes.description: Description text shown when the table has no data.

customElement.summary: A configurable component for displaying tabular data.

label: Tab Panel
summary: Content panel for tabs.
overview: TabPanel contains the content associated with a tab.

customElement.name: TabPanel
customElement.summary: Content panel for tabs.

label: Tabs
summary: Container for tab components.
overview: Tabs holds the tab buttons and manages their layout.

customElement.name: Tabs
customElement.summary: Container for tab components.

label: Tag
summary: Tag component for displaying labels or values with optional dismiss functionality.

customElement.name: Tag

members.name: dismissible
members.description: If true, the tag will have a close icon.

members.name: color
members.description: Tag color.

attributes.name: dismissible
attributes.description: If true, the tag will have a close icon.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Tag color.

customElement.summary: Tag component for displaying labels or values with optional dismiss functionality.

label: Textarea
summary: The Textarea component is used to capture user input.

customElement.name: Textarea

members.name: helperText
members.description: Helper text to display below the input.

members.name: dirty
members.description:
Returns true when the text field has been interacted with. Native
validation errors only display in response to user interactions.

attributes.name: helper-text
attributes.description: Helper text to display below the input.

customElement.summary: The Textarea component is used to capture user input.

label: Time Picker
summary: The Time Picker component is used to capture time user input.

customElement.name: TimePicker

members.name: helperText
members.description: Helper text to display below the input.

members.name: dirty
members.description:
Returns true when the text field has been interacted with. Native
validation errors only display in response to user interactions.

attributes.name: helper-text
attributes.description: Helper text to display below the input.

customElement.summary: The Time Picker component is used to capture time user input.

label: Tooltip
summary: Displays a tooltip for an element.

customElement.name: Tooltip

members.name: for
members.description:
The ID of the element the tooltip should attach to.
If not provided, it defaults to the parent element.

attributes.name: for
attributes.description:
The ID of the element the tooltip should attach to.
If not provided, it defaults to the parent element.

customElement.summary: Displays a tooltip for an element.

label: Tree Node
summary: A tree node represents a single item in a hierarchical tree structure. It supports nesting, icons, links, and keyboard navigation.

customElement.name: TreeNode

members.name: value
members.description: The value used to identify this node when selected.

members.name: label
members.description: The display label for this node.

members.name: icon
members.description: Optional icon name to display before the label.

members.name: href
members.description: Optional hyperlink to navigate to on click.

members.name: target
members.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the node.

members.name: selected
members.description: Whether the node is currently selected.

members.name: expanded
members.description: Whether child nodes are visible.

members.name: level
members.description: The nesting depth level (set automatically by the parent tree-view).

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The value used to identify this node when selected.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The display label for this node.

attributes.name: icon

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: Optional hyperlink to navigate to on click.

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the node.

attributes.name: selected
attributes.description: Whether the node is currently selected.

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description: Whether child nodes are visible.

attributes.name: level
attributes.description: The nesting depth level (set automatically by the parent tree-view).

customElement.summary: A tree node represents a single item in a hierarchical tree structure. It supports nesting, icons, links, and keyboard navigation.

label: Tree View
summary: A tree view is a hierarchical structure that provides nested levels of navigation. It supports keyboard navigation, single/multi select, and expandable nodes.

customElement.name: TreeView

members.name: selectedNode
members.description: The value of the currently selected node.

attributes.name: selected-node
attributes.description: The value of the currently selected node.

customElement.summary: A tree view is a hierarchical structure that provides nested levels of navigation. It supports keyboard navigation, single/multi select, and expandable nodes.
